pages.text: Kopano and Rea are going to town for the first time ever!

blocks.text: Kopano and Rea are going to town for the first time ever!

pages.text: “Remember to fasten your seatbelts,” Mama says. “Auntie Boi has sent you girls a gift and we’re going to collect it at the big post office in town.” “We can’t wait to see the gift Mama,” the girls say and off they go.

blocks.text: “Remember to fasten your seatbelts,” Mama says.
“Auntie Boi has sent you girls a gift and we’re going to collect it at the big post office in town.”
“We can’t wait to see the gift Mama,” the girls say and off they go.

pages.text: “The Nelson Mandela Bridge leads us into town,” Mama says. “Mhhmm, Nelson Mandela Bridge,” Kopano says.

blocks.text: “The Nelson Mandela Bridge leads us into town,” Mama says.
“Mhhmm, Nelson Mandela Bridge,” Kopano says.

pages.text: “Look,” Rea says to Kopano, “so many tall buildings.” “Mhhmm, do you think our gift is tall too Rea?” Kopano asks.

blocks.text: “Look,” Rea says to Kopano, “so many tall buildings.”
“Mhhmm, do you think our gift is tall too Rea?” Kopano asks.

pages.text: “Look,” Kopano says to Rea, “so many fruits and vegetables.” “Mhhmm, do you think our gift tastes yummy?” Rea asks Kopano. FRUITS AND VEGETABLES

blocks.text: “Look,” Kopano says to Rea, “so many fruits and vegetables.”
“Mhhmm, do you think our gift tastes yummy?” Rea asks Kopano.
FRUITS AND VEGETABLES

pages.text: “Look,” Rea says to Kopano, “traditional clothes!” “Mhhmm, do you think Auntie Boi bought us traditional clothes?” Kopano asks.

blocks.text: “Look,” Rea says to Kopano, “traditional clothes!”
“Mhhmm, do you think Auntie Boi bought us traditional clothes?” Kopano asks.
OPEN

pages.text: “Wow, it’s like one big party,” Kopano and Rea say. “Maybe your gift sings a loud song,” Mama says smiling. “Mhhmm, we love loud songs,” the girls say.

blocks.text: OPEN
“Wow, it’s like one big party,” Kopano and Rea say.
“Maybe your gift sings a loud song,” Mama says smiling.
“Mhhmm, we love loud songs,” the girls say.

pages.text: “We are here!” Mama says. POST OFFICE

blocks.text: “We are here!” Mama says.
POST OFFICE
OPEN

pages.text: SHAKE! SHAKE! RATTLE! SHAKE! “Do you think it’s tall like the buildings we saw?” Rea asks. “No,” Kopano says. Rea takes the box and smells it. “Do you think it tastes yummy like the fruit and veg we saw?” “No,” Rea says. Kopano shakes the box again. “It doesn’t make a loud noise like the speakers we passed.” “And either,” I don’t Rea says. think it’s traditional clothes

blocks.text: SHAKE!
SHAKE!
RATTLE!
SHAKE!
“Do you think it’s tall like the buildings we saw?” Rea asks.
“No,” Kopano says. Rea takes the box and smells it.
“Do you think it tastes yummy like the fruit and veg we saw?”
“No,” Rea says.
Kopano shakes the box again. “It doesn’t make a loud noise like the speakers we passed.”
“And either,” I don’t Rea says. think it’s traditional clothes

pages.text: Kopano and Rea sit down right there on the floor of the post office and open the box.

blocks.text: Kopano and Rea sit down right there on the floor of the post office and open the box.

pages.text: “Those are Maasai dolls,” Mama says to the girls. “Maasai dolls from Kenya,” Kopano says. “We love these dolls!” Rea says.

blocks.text: “Those are Maasai dolls,” Mama says to the girls.
“Maasai dolls from Kenya,” Kopano says.
“We love these dolls!” Rea says.

pages.text: Mama takes a picture of the girls and sends it to Auntie Boi.

blocks.text: Mama takes a picture of the girls and sends it to Auntie Boi.
